2606.02625.title: DXA-Derived Skeletal Phenotypes and Hip Fracture Risk: A Backdoor-Adjusted Causal Analysis
2606.02625.paper_summary: Purpose: To compare dual-energy X-ray absorptiometry (DXA)-derived hip skeletal phenotypes in relation to hip fracture risk using prespecified confounder adjustment and to assess whether phenotypes ranked by their backdoor-adjusted average treatment effects (ATEs) improve risk stratification. Methods: We analyzed 21,098 UK Biobank participants with linked health records, hip DXA-derived skeletal measures, and prespecified covariates. Sixteen phenotypes spanning bone mineral content (BMC), bone mineral density (BMD), and T-score across hip-related regions were evaluated. Confounder selection was guided by a prespecified directed acyclic graph (DAG). Backdoor-adjusted ATEs were estimated on the absolute risk-difference scale per standard deviation (SD) increase. Effect heterogeneity was evaluated for total femur BMD, and downstream prediction was assessed using clinical variables combined with phenotypes ranked by ATE magnitude. Results: Among 21,098 participants, 115 had hip fractures. All 16 phenotypes showed negative backdoor-adjusted ATEs per SD increase. The largest ATEs were observed for total femur BMC and total femur BMD, each with a risk difference of -0.0047, corresponding to approximately 4.7 fewer hip fractures per 1,000 participants per SD higher phenotype value. Conditional effects of total femur BMD were stronger among older participants and those with lower BMI. In prediction, clinical variables plus the top 11 ATE-ranked phenotypes achieved higher AUC than FRAX with femoral neck BMD (0.842 vs. 0.709), with higher sensitivity (0.748 vs. 0.443) and similar specificity (0.793 vs. 0.777). Conclusion: DXA-derived hip skeletal phenotypes differed in their backdoor-adjusted ATEs. Phenotype-level causal evaluation may help identify informative DXA measures for risk stratification.
2606.02625.paper_summary_zh: 目的：比較雙能X光吸收測量（DXA）衍生的髖部骨骼表型與髖部骨折風險之間的關係，使用預先指定的混淆因子調整，並評估按其背門調整的平均處理效應（ATE）排名的表型是否能改善風險分層。<lb/>方法：我們分析了21,098名擁有連結健康記錄、髖部DXA衍生骨骼測量和預先指定協變數的英國生物銀行參與者。<lb/>評估了涵蓋骨礦物質含量（BMC）、骨礦物質密度（BMD）和髖部相關區域的T-score的16種表型。<lb/>混淆因子的選擇由預先指定的有向無環圖（DAG）指導。<lb/>在每個標準差（SD）增加的絕對風險差異尺度上估計背門調整的ATE。<lb/>對總股骨BMD的效應異質性進行了評估，並使用臨床變數結合按ATE大小排名的表型評估下游預測。<lb/>結果：在21,098名參與者中，有115人發生了髖部骨折。<lb/>所有16種表型在每個SD增加時均顯示出負的背門調整ATE。<lb/>總股骨BMC和總股骨BMD的最大ATE分別為-0.0047，對應於每1,000名參與者中每SD較高表型值約少4.7次髖部骨折。<lb/>在年長參與者和BMI較低者中，總股骨BMD的條件效應更強。<lb/>在預測中，臨床變數加上排名前11的ATE表型的AUC高於使用股骨頸BMD的FRAX（0.842對0.709），且靈敏度更高（0.748對0.443），特異性相似（0.793對0.777）。<lb/>結論：DXA衍生的髖部骨骼表型在其背門調整的ATE中存在差異。<lb/>表型層級的因果評估可能有助於識別有助於風險分層的DXA測量。
2606.02625.author: Zixin Shi et.al.
2606.02625.authors: Zixin Shi, Chen Zhao, Meiling Zhou, Kevin A. Maupin, Joyce H. Keyak, Nancy E. Lane, Kuan-Jui Su, Hui Shen, Hong-Wen Deng, Kui Zhang, Weihua Zhou
